creator: isaac@waldronis.com
text:
This port forces you to set some settings through variables in rc.conf because it does not provide a way to unset or disable setting them through the rc variables.  For example, a default value is set for the transmission_download_dir variable but there is no way to unset this so that the value in transmission's settings.json file will be honored.  To reproduce this behavior:

1. remove any transmission_download_dir setting in /etc/rc.conf
2. stop the transmission service
3. edit transmission's settings.json file and set the download-dir setting to some non-standard location
4. start the transmission service

At this point settings.json will be overwritten with the default value set in transmission's rc.d script.  Replacing step 1 in the above procedure with 'set transmission_download="NO" in rc.conf', as is the common practice for disabling settings in rc.conf, results in the download directory being set to the literal "NO."  I would prefer that a setting of "NO" translate to the -w flag being left out of transmission_flags so that the value of download-dir in settings.json is retained on starting the daemon.
